data.title: 'Fix Your AI': Elon Musk Rips Into Anthropic after $380B Valuation
data.description:
AI startup Anthropic raised $30 billion in Series G funding, giving the company a post-money valuation of $380 billion. Despite the funding milestone, Elon Musk sharply criticized Anthropic's AI, telling the company to "Fix it." Musk, who runs the rival AI firm xAI, posted on X that Claude AI "hates Whites & Asians, especially Chinese, heterosexuals, and men," calling it "misanthropic and evil."

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk has previously also pointed out the irony in Anthropic's name, saying the human-focused AI company has ended up producing technology he calls "misanthropic."

Anthropic's New Funding Round

Anthropic's latest Series G funding round was led by GIC and Coatue. The round was co-led by D. E. Shaw Ventures, Dragoneer, Founders Fund, ICONIQ, and MGX.

In its official announcement, Anthropic said the funding will help boost its research, product development, and infrastructure, reinforcing its role as a leader in enterprise AI and coding. The company also noted that in less than three years since earning its first dollar in revenue, its run-rate revenue has already reached $14 billion, growing more than tenfold each year.

Musk vs. Anthropic

Anthropic is best known for its Claude large language models and competes directly with xAI's chatbot Grok. Musk has been critical of Anthropic before, with reports suggesting the startup cut off xAI's access to Claude models.

This latest confrontation also highlights rising tensions in the AI industry, where companies race to develop powerful systems while facing scrutiny over bias and safety. In this competitive landscape, success increasingly depends not only on technological breakthroughs but also on public battles among industry leaders.

Interestingly, Anthropic's rapid valuation growth reflects strong investor appetite for leading AI developers. By contrast, despite Musk's push, market data shows xAI remains a smaller player globally, with generative AI traffic still dominated by OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's (GOOGL) Gemini.

Which Is the Best AI Stock, According to Analysts?

Most of the leading AI companies are still private, which limits direct investing options. Still, investors can gain exposure through publicly traded players tied to the AI ecosystem. data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Fear Robinhood's Post-Earnings Slump? Analysts Say 'Frustrating' But Stay Bullish
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD), the popular retail trading platform, saw its stock plunge nearly 17% over the past two days after mixed Q4 results on February 10. Earnings per share (EPS) of $0.66 beat analysts' consensus estimate of $0.63 per share, but sales of $1.28 billion missed the $1.35 billion consensus forecast. The shortfall mainly came from a 38% drop in crypto transaction revenue to $221 million, driven by lower trading volumes.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HOOD shares are down 37% year-to-date. The key question: Will this downward spiral continue, or are downside risks easing? Analysts offer a mixed but mostly optimistic view.

Analysts Trim Targets But Stay Bullish

Truist Financial analyst David Smith cut his price target from $130 to $120, still implying 68.7% upside potential, but kept his Buy rating. He called the Q4 report "frustrating," stating that outside of prediction markets, Robinhood had few positives. Key numbers like user growth and net deposits were weak in December alongside crypto weakness, while January was a bit better. He remains optimistic about Robinhood's long-term potential, despite short-term volatility.

Similarly, Needham analyst John Todaro slashed his price target to $100 (from $135), suggesting 40.6% upside, while keeping his Buy view. He noted that Q4 results were strong and met his estimates. Prediction markets particularly stood out, with January volumes hitting a record $3.5 billion in contracts. However, crypto volumes and take rates weakened in fiscal 26, along with options and equities. He expects crypto weakness to linger for two quarters before recovering.

Additionally, Piper Sandler analyst Patrick Moley kept his Buy rating but trimmed his price target from $155 to $135, implying 89.8% upside. Moley sees short-term headwinds but keeps his long-term view intact. "If you can stomach the volatility, HOOD is the best way to play secular growth in retail trading and the closest FinTech platform we've ever seen to achieving 'super app' status," Moley argued.

Key Takeaway

Analysts' concerns mostly center around short-term crypto and trading volume volatility, which make up a big chunk of Robinhood's revenue (crypto alone was about 17% of Q4 total). Yet, all three analysts see strong long-term potential in Robinhood's low-cost brokerage, expanding products like prediction markets, and retail trading growth.

Is HOOD a Long-Term Investment?

On TipRanks, HOOD stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Robinhood Markets price target of $135.46 implies 90.5% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Rivian Stock (RIVN) Surges 15% after Earnings — What Excited Investors?
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN), the electric-vehicle maker, jumped about 15% in after-hours trading Thursday after reporting fourth-quarter results and giving an update on its upcoming R2 vehicle and delivery outlook.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company reported about $1.29 billion in revenue and a smaller-than-expected loss per share of $0.70. It also posted a second straight quarter of gross profit. However, investors focused more on the company's outlook, especially updates on its upcoming R2 vehicle and delivery guidance.

What Stood Out This Quarter

Rivian said its lower-priced R2 SUV remains on track for customer deliveries in the second quarter of 2026. Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe called the launch a "key inflection" for the company, since the vehicle is meant to reach a much larger group of buyers than its current R1T pickup and R1S SUV.

The company also guided 2026 deliveries to about 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles, pointing to growth after a largely flat year. For EV makers, delivery outlook often matters more than a single quarter's earnings because it shows future demand. A more affordable vehicle could help increase production and improve manufacturing efficiency as Rivian expands output.

At the same time, the results showed some operating progress. Rivian recorded gross profit for a second straight quarter, helped in part by software and services revenue linked to its partnership with Volkswagen (VWAGY).

This indicates the company is beginning to generate income beyond vehicle sales, which investors often watch as a sign of improving business stability.

What Investors Will Watch Next

Looking ahead, the focus shifts to execution. Rivian still expects adjusted EBITDA losses of about $1.8 billion to $2.1 billion in 2026 and plans around $2 billion in spending as it prepares for the R2 launch.

Investors will watch whether the company can ramp production smoothly and meet its delivery guidance of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles. The early stages of the R2 rollout matter, since higher volume is needed to lower costs per vehicle.

They will also track cash use and margins in the coming quarters. The recent gross profit and rising software and services revenue were positive signs, but the key question is whether higher production can slowly reduce losses and move the company toward profit.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on seven Buys, four Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $17.75 per share implies 26.8% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Did All "Magnificent Seven" Stocks Fall Today?
data.description:
All seven mega-cap technology companies — Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Tesla (TSLA) — closed lower on Thursday as investors turned cautious toward big tech and artificial-intelligence spending.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Apple drew the most attention after reports said its planned AI upgrade to Siri may be delayed. The stock fell about 5%, its worst day since April 2025. Because Apple is one of the largest weights in major indexes, its drop pulled sentiment down across the group. Investors are also watching whether new AI features can drive the next iPhone upgrade cycle, making any delay more sensitive for the stock.

There was broader evidence of caution as well. UBS recently downgraded the U.S. technology sector to Neutral, warning that heavy AI spending and uncertainty around software demand could pressure near-term returns.

Investors are also watching rising spending plans. Mark Hawtin of Liontrust Asset Management (LIO) recently said the rapid increase in capital expenditure across the "Magnificent Seven" could become a risk, as companies invest heavily in AI while current revenue from the technology remains limited. He cited Amazon as an example, noting much of its expected cash flow this year could be absorbed by rising capital spending.

What the Group Decline Signals

When these companies fall together, it usually means investors are reducing risk in the market's most crowded trade. The "Magnificent Seven" have powered a large share of market gains over the past two years, so weakness in the group can weigh on the wider indexes.

The move does not point to weak earnings. Instead, it reflects concern about future growth — specifically how quickly artificial intelligence will translate into profits for the biggest technology companies.

Which Magnificent 7 Stock Is the Better Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, out of the Magnificent 7 stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Microsoft stock has the most room to run. In fact, Microsoft's average price target of $593.38 per share implies 47.7% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Stock (AAPL) Plunges 5% in Worst Day Since April — And It Wasn't Just Siri
data.description:
Apple (AAPL), the iPhone maker and the world's most valuable public company, fell about 5% on Thursday, marking its worst trading day since April 2025. The drop erased the stock's gains for the year and left it down roughly 4% in 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The decline followed a Bloomberg report that ai-launch">Apple's planned AI upgrade to Siri may be delayed. The rollout has reportedly been pushed to May or later instead of arriving in the coming weeks. However, Apple said it still expects to release the features in 2026.

But the reaction on Wall Street suggested the concern was larger than a simple product delay.

Investors have been waiting to see Apple's AI plan. While many tech companies have already launched new AI tools, Apple has moved more slowly. Because of that, even a short delay raised worries the company could fall behind rivals in adding new features to its devices.

The Second Problem Appeared the Same Day

On the same day, Apple also drew regulatory attention. It was accused of "censoring conservatives" in its news feed. Federal Trade Commission Chair Andrew Ferguson sent CEO Tim Cook a letter asking the company to review how its Apple News app selects and presents content after reports of potential bias.

The development did not directly affect sales, but it added another uncertainty for investors already reacting to the Siri news.

Why the Market Reacted So Strongly

The sell-off also came as sentiment toward large technology companies has recently weakened. Many investors now question whether heavy AI spending will pay off soon. Software stocks have already fallen in recent days, and analysts recently cut their view on the U.S. tech sector.

Apple actually reported strong earnings last month. But markets care more about future growth than past results. For Apple, that growth depends on whether AI features can drive new iPhone upgrades.

So Thursday's fall was not just about a delayed feature. The market was reacting to a bigger worry, that Apple's next growth driver, AI, may take longer than investors expected.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 27 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $306.89 implies 17.25% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Stock (AAPL) Sinks on Reports It Might Pushback Siri AI Launch
data.description:
Apple's (AAPL) stock is down 4% on reports that the company might delay the launch of its artificial intelligence-enabled Siri digital assistant.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The consumer electronics giant had been planning to include a number of AI upgrades and enhancements to Siri in a March operating system update, but the company is now reconsidering, according to multiple media reports.

Apple's senior management team is now considering announcing an AI Siri that can tap into personal data and on-screen content this September when iOS 27 is launched alongside the iPhone 18. The postponement reportedly comes after testing uncovered problems with the AI software used with the Siri digital assistant.

Apple's AI Woes

Media reports say that Siri didn't properly process AI queries or took too long to respond to questions it received from users. Apple has received heavy criticism in recent years for not having a more robust AI strategy, and for not integrating AI into its devices, especially its signature iPhone.

On the positive side, Apple's iPhone sales reached a record last quarter even without a major AI integration. And the company has largely avoided the massive capital expenditures that other technology giants such as Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) are spending on AI.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 27 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $306.89 implies 12.26% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/12/26? Inflation Report Ahead
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded higher late Thursday night after a weak day on Wall Street, while traders waited for a key inflation report due Friday morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.23%, 0.09%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 9:48 p.m. EDT on February 12.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In Thursday's session, the major indexes fell as worries about artificial-intelligence disruption spread to sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software. The S&P 500 dropped about 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite fell roughly 2%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost nearly 670 points, or 1.3%.

In after-hours trading, Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped 11% after strong earnings and guidance. Airbnb (ABNB) gained around 2% on upbeat outlook comments, while Pinterest (PINS) slid 14% following weak quarterly results and a soft forecast.

All of the "Magnificent Seven" technology stocks finished lower. Cisco Systems (CSCO) fell 12% after disappointing guidance, and Apple (AAPL) dropped 5%, its biggest one-day decline since April 2025.

Investors are now focused on Friday's consumer price index report. Economists expect inflation to rise 2.5% from a year ago and 0.3% from the prior month.

data.title: Nova Lake Die Sizes Leak Sends Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Sliding
data.description:
Some interesting news slipped out recently about chip stock Intel (INTC), specifically about the Nova Lake processor lineup. Intel has a lot riding on the Nova Lake processor, which makes the fact that a leak emerged about these chips all the more unsettling. The news that slipped out focused on die sizes, and gave an advance look at the Nova Lake beyond what we have heard so far. Investors were not pleased, though, as evidenced by a nearly 3% loss in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new word suggests that the Nova Lake line will have eight Coyote Cove P-cores along with 32 Arctic Wolf E-cores. That means around 110 square millimeters, which is quite a step from Nova Lake's predecessor. That processor measured about 150 square millimeters. That, coupled with the fact that the Nova Lake will be made using TSMC's (TSM) N2 process, means that the Nova Lake line will likely not be inexpensive to make.

It will help that most laptop Nova Lake processors will be made in-house at Intel's Arizona fab. And given that laptop processors outsell desktop processors by roughly seven to three these days, the costs may not be all that egregious after all. Still, knowing what we know so far suggests that higher prices than expected may show up later.

Wedbush Likes It

New reports suggest a reason to like Intel beyond that, though, as Intel is looking to put a $100 million commitment into SambaNova Systems. This might not sound like a great idea on the surface, given how aggressively Intel has been pinching pennies of late. But Wedbush analysts suggest that this could be the start of something truly impressive.

Reports note that Wedbush is calling the move a "…meaningful step in reshaping Intel's role in high-end compute." Given that a lot of computing power is moving to artificial intelligence, and SambaNova has something of a presence therein, Intel's connection to SambaNova could fuel substantial advancement in the higher-end computing sector. The fact that Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has a personal connection to SambaNova, however, may taint the deal a bit.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 20 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 100.12% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.32 per share implies 3.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Are Memory Chip Stocks Rising Today? SanDisk (SNDK) Leads the Gain
data.description:
Memory Chip Stocks, SanDisk (SNDK), Micron (MU), Western Digital (WDC), and Seagate Technology (STX), all closed higher on Thursday after Japan-based memory maker Kioxia gave strong guidance and pointed to continued demand from AI data centers.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kioxia, a producer of NAND flash storage chips, forecast full-year sales and operating income above analyst expectations. The company also said some customers are already trying to secure supply for 2027 and 2028, even though memory contracts are normally signed about one year in advance.

The company's finance chief added that tight supply is leading to a sharp rise in selling prices.

What the News Signals for Memory Prices

Memory companies' earnings depend heavily on chip prices. Over the past year, profits improved because supply was tight and prices stayed high. Kioxia's comments now suggest that shortage may last longer than expected.

In addition, NAND flash is widely used in solid-state drives inside cloud servers. As companies continue building AI infrastructure, they need large storage capacity to hold training data and outputs. Because customers are securing supply years in advance, it suggests they expect demand to remain strong.

What Pushed These Memory Stocks Higher

SanDisk moved the most, closing more than 5% higher, because it manufactures NAND chips with Kioxia through a joint venture and is directly exposed to pricing. Micron also gained after recently saying shipments of its next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips began earlier than planned.

Western Digital and Seagate, which sell storage products used in data centers, often move with expectations for memory pricing.

Memory stocks had cooled earlier this year after a strong rally, but the latest update reassured investors that elevated chip prices, which support profits, may continue for now.
